sutta: on a certain occasion the exalted 1 was staying near sabahti in jaita grove in anathapindi kaspar now at that time at savathi a certain monk had been bitten by a snake and had died so a great number of monks came to visit the exalted 1 and on coming to him saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated those mounts said this to the exalted 1 lord a certain monk here in savathi has been bitten by a snake and has died and the buddha said then monks that monk did not suffuse with the heart of goodwill mehta the 4 royal families of snakes had he done so that monk would not have died of snake bite what are the 4 royal families of snakes the royal family of snakes called virupaka that called erapata that called chabyaputta and the royal family of snakes called kanta gotamaka monks that monk did not suffuse with heart of goodwill these 4 royal families of snakes had he done so he would not have died of snake bite monks for self-warding for self-guarding for self-protection i do enjoin that you suffuse with the heart of goodwill these 4 royal snakes of these 4 royal families of snakes thus [Music] towards the full 2-footed meta meter towards the 4-footed towards the many-footed meta may the footless do me no harm may not the 2-footed dummy arm may the 4-footed no harm may not the many footed do me harm all creatures all that breathe all beings may they all see what is auspicious may no evil come to them messelus is the buddha the dhamma magelus the sangha limited are creeping things snakes scorpions centipedes spiders lizards and rats made by me is this protection made by me as this paritta may those beings go away homage to the exalted 1 homage to the 7 samasan buddhas and that's the end of the suta
commentary: the last part that i read out to you is the parita called the kanda parita go something like this [Music] this is this suitcase about this parita now this manga he got killed by this normally snakes don't bite us normally unless they are cornered unless they are threatened their life is threatened then only they buy this for example sometimes you walk in the dump you don't have a torchlight you don't know you step on them and then it's likely that they will bite you and now if they bite you uh how hard they bite you how much poison goes into your blood depends on conditions it's just like imagine for example you step on a dog if it's your own dog you step on the tail or you step on the foot now it will naturally try to bite you because it is in pain but if it is your own dog even though it bites you he doesn't bite very hard but if somebody else's dog you're not familiar with you step on the tail and you come and bite you very hard so in the same way how hard the snake bites you and depends on whether you have meta or not so if we have meta we have a lot of good will at the snake they can sense also so uh when they bite us it won't be very hard that's why the buddha said that's important to have meta there's another suta somewhere else also where the buddha said if we have meta loving kindness of good will towards all living beings then wherever we go we are not disturbed by these spirits whether they are ghosts or deivas they won't disturb us because they can read our minds they know what type of mind we have so we have a mind of a good will towards all beings it is always for our protection so this this the buddha taught his disciples to chant this kanda parita especially if we go to lonely secluded places and suppose you stay in a cave where there's likely to be snakes and male uh possibly spirits so we chant this uh this is a very very useful paritana besides the 4 royal snake families now you give meta towards fruitless beings that means snakes ghosts etc and then towards 2 footed beings like human beings and towards 4-footed beings like dogs etc and then towards many footed beings like centipedes scorpions etc then he said may the footless do me no harm may not the 2-footed do me harm etc so if they ask if they are psychic if they are spirits when they listen to this they know that you don't mean them any harm and you don't want also to be harmed so it is very useful parita for forest monks forest monks like to chant this paratha wherever we go somewhere when we feel a bit afraid then the nexus is
